senses.definition: an extinct African wild donkey with a distinctive striped pattern on the front half of its body
examples: The quagga once roamed the plains of South Africa.
Researchers study paintings of quaggas to learn more about their appearance.

senses.definition: plural of quagga, referring to multiple individuals of the extinct zebra-like mammal
examples: Museum displays include drawings and bones of quaggas.
Quaggas are studied to understand variation in coat patterns among zebras.

senses.definition: more quagga-like in appearance, especially having more striped patterns
examples: The painting shows a quaggier animal than earlier drawings.
The stripes on the head make it look quaggier.

senses.definition: most quagga-like in appearance, with the most pronounced striped pattern
examples: The quaggiest illustration is the most zebra-like in the exhibit.
In the artist's series, this piece is the quaggiest.

senses.definition: the quality of being quagga-like or having zebra-like stripes
examples: The quagginess of the animal is evident in the frontal stripes.
Her drawing captures the quagginess of the extinct species.

senses.definition: plural of quagginess; multiple instances of being quagga-like
examples: Descriptions highlight various quagginesses in the sketches.
The notes discuss differences in quagginesses across depictions.

senses.definition: to work or move slowly and with difficulty; to struggle or flounder
examples: They quaggled through the mud to reach the riverbank.
The project began to quaggle as funding dried up.

senses.definition: soft and damp or muddy; spongy
examples: The ground was quaggy after the rain.
We waded through quaggy fields to reach the trail.

senses.definition: a soft, wet ground that sucks things in; a difficult or dangerous situation
examples: The hikers got stuck in a quagmire of mud.
The budget crisis created a political quagmire.

senses.definition: stuck in mud or in a difficult situation
examples: The vehicle was quagmired after the storm.
They found themselves quagmired in negotiations.

senses.definition: plural of quagmire; multiple difficult or sticky situations
examples: Environmental regulations have created several quagmires for industries.
They navigated through various quagmires during the talks.

senses.definition: more quagmire-like in nature; more difficult or ensnaring
examples: The legal case grew quagmirier as new evidence emerged.
This route becomes quagmirier after rainfall.

senses.definition: most quagmire-like; the most difficult or entangling
examples: This is the quagmiriest situation we've faced.
The negotiations reached the quagmiriest phase.

senses.definition: the act of getting stuck in a quagmire or becoming mired in difficulties
examples: The project is quagmiring as deadlines slip.
We are quagmiring in red tape.

senses.definition: resembling or characteristic of a quagmire; muddy or boggy
examples: The field was quagmiry after the rain.
Her notes describe a quagmiry landscape.

senses.definition: slang or informal plural for quagmire-like problems; or short for quags (quintet of something) depending on context
examples: The team faced several quags in the project.
We tried to avoid the quags during planning.

senses.definition: plural of quahog; a hard-shelled edible clam native to North America
examples: They collected quahaugs from the shore.
Quahaugs are used in chowder and other seafood dishes.

senses.definition: a large edible clam; the shellfish known as the hard clam
examples: We bought fresh quahogs for the seafood boil.
Quahogs are popular in New England chowder.

senses.definition: plural of quahog; multiple hard clams
examples: The market sold fresh quahogs and lobsters.
They steamed several quahogs for dinner.

senses.definition: a dock or platform for loading and unloading ships; a quay
examples: The ship pulled up to the quai at dawn.
They walked along the quai to watch the boats.

senses.definition: a shallow Scottish ceremonial drinking cup with two handles
examples: They passed the quaich around the table.
The quaich is traditional in Scottish hospitality ceremonies.

senses.definition: plural of quaich; multiple Scottish drinking cups
examples: The collection includes several antique quaiches.
They polished the quaiches for the ceremony.

senses.definition: plural of quaich; multiple Scottish drinking cups
examples: He received two quaichs as wedding gifts.
The quaichs were engraved with family crests.

senses.definition: an obsolete surname or variation; can be a place or person name
examples: The family name Quaife appears in historical records.
Quaife is mentioned in old maps as a family surname.
